_doc: Calibrated selectors for the Square Item Sales report (https://app.squareup.com/dashboard/sales/reports/item-sales), used by AKSHAYA's refresh pipeline. Sibling of dashboard.json (Sales Summary). Login flow is identical and is owned by dashboard.json's `login` section.
verified_by: AKSHAYA refresh, end-to-end via user-playwright MCP

date_picker.pill_button_role: button with name like 'May 2026' (current month label). Has [expanded] attribute when picker is open.
date_picker.start_textbox_format: MM/DD/YYYY — e.g. '04/22/2026'. Element resolves to '#ember<NN>'; ref changes per snapshot. Use the textbox role under the 'Start' label inside the open picker.
date_picker.end_textbox_format: MM/DD/YYYY — e.g. '05/11/2026'. Element resolves to '#ember<NN+2>' (NN+2 because of an interleaved separator element).
date_picker.submission: After filling both textboxes, press Enter on the keyboard. The picker closes, the report header updates to e.g. 'Apr 22, 2026 – May 11, 2026', and the table reloads.
date_picker.notes: End date is INCLUSIVE — a range of 04/22/2026 → 05/11/2026 captures through end-of-day 5/11. The downloaded CSV filename embeds the exclusive end as `items-2026-04-22-2026-05-12.csv` (Square's convention).

export.trigger_button_role: button with name 'Export', collapsed by default. Located in top-right of the report header area.
menu_options_after_click: Summary CSV — daily/weekly aggregates only; NOT what AKSHAYA needs
Detail CSV Export — row-per-transaction, has Date/Time/Item/Qty/Transaction ID/Payment ID columns. This is what `forecast_v2.load_square_orders` expects.
export.csv_naming_pattern: items-{start_yyyy_mm_dd}-{end_plus_1_yyyy_mm_dd}.csv (Square uses exclusive end date in the filename)
export.save_directory: Jarvis/extracted/downloads/ (default Playwright profile download dir); move to `get open/playground/` for forecast pipeline pickup

table_layout_reference.total_row_position: bottom of the table, separate generic with 'Total' first cell
